name: International Existing Building Code 2021
copyright_notice: International Existing Building Code® 2021 © 2021 International Code Council, Inc.

compliance_method.label: IEBC Compliance Method
compliance_method.description: IEBC offers three compliance paths (Chapters 4, 5–13, or 14). Choose one per project.

rules.label: Investigation & Evaluation
rules.notes_template: Verify a §302 investigation/evaluation report has been prepared documenting existing conditions, structural capacity, and code-required upgrades.
rules.citation: IEBC 2021 §302 – An evaluation of the existing building is required to determine the scope of work and applicable code requirements.
rules.remediation: Prepare an existing-conditions evaluation including structural, fire-protection, and accessibility findings.

rules.label: Accessibility – Existing Buildings
rules.notes_template: Verify alterations comply with §305: at least 20% of altered area construction cost spent on accessible path of travel to the primary function area, restrooms, and drinking fountains (until accessible).
rules.citation: IEBC 2021 §305.7 – Alterations to a primary function area trigger an accessible path of travel obligation up to 20% of the construction cost.
rules.remediation: Plan path-of-travel upgrades: entrance, route, restrooms, drinking fountains, public phones, until accessible or 20% of cost is reached.

rules.label: Prescriptive Compliance Method
rules.notes_template: Apply Chapter 4 prescriptive scoping for additions, alterations, and change of occupancy; structural §403, accessibility §410, etc.
rules.citation: IEBC 2021 Chapter 4 – Prescriptive method for repairs, additions, alterations, and change of occupancy.
rules.remediation: Document each work category with the Chapter 4 requirements applied.

rules.label: Work-Area Compliance Method
rules.notes_template: Classify the work as Repair, Level 1/2/3 Alteration, Change of Occupancy, Addition, or Historic; apply the corresponding chapter (5–13).
rules.citation: IEBC 2021 Chapters 5–13 – Work-area method classifies work and applies a tailored set of requirements.
rules.remediation: Identify level of alteration and apply Chapters 7–10 requirements; combine with Chapter 11 (Change of Occupancy) and Chapter 12 (Additions) where applicable.

rules.label: Performance Compliance Method
rules.notes_template: Apply Chapter 14 scoring system covering fire safety, means of egress, and general safety; aggregate scores must equal/exceed minimums in Table 1401.6.
rules.citation: IEBC 2021 Chapter 14 – Performance method scores fire safety, means of egress, and general safety against acceptance criteria.
rules.remediation: Complete the Chapter 14 evaluation worksheet and demonstrate aggregate scores meet acceptance thresholds.
